overrideJustification.summary: User manually verified the end-user behavior for VAL-SPAWN-016 in the live dev server on :5720 after commit c6bf365: 'I verified visually, the agents are visible in the sidebar and the full agent messages appear when clicking them.' The scrutiny reviewer's remaining concern (OffscreenClient.currentMessageId not reconciled at selection) is a theoretical timing edge case — post-click text_delta forking a second message, or post-click tool_end/response_done being dropped — that was not reproduced during manual verification. Automated test suite is 2950 / 2 skipped green. The tech debt is tracked as a non-blocking fix feature `misc-reconcile-offscreen-client-currentmessageid-on-selection` in a new `misc-hydration` milestone scheduled for a later hardening pass. See features.json for that entry. Proceeding to user-testing validator for core-followup-2.

remainingTechDebt.tracks: Reconcile OffscreenClient.currentMessageId to the hydrated in-progress assistant message when selectScoop loads an active session.

blockingIssues.description: Selecting a running bridge scoop mid-turn still hydrates persisted pre-click state without reconnecting OffscreenClient.currentMessageId to that in-progress assistant turn. Post-click text deltas can fork a second message, while post-click tool_end/response_done events can be dropped entirely, so c6bf365 does not fully close VAL-SPAWN-016 and fix-agent-bridge-chat-panel-wiring remains unresolved.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/architecture.md to record that VAL-SPAWN-016 requires both per-event SessionStore persistence and selection-time reconnection of OffscreenClient.currentMessageId to the hydrated in-progress message.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: For extension-side chat hydration fixes, require a regression test or manual verification that selects a running scoop after pre-click partial/tool-start state has been persisted, then asserts post-click text_delta, tool_end, and response_done continue the same message instead of creating or dropping events.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Round-2 scrutiny for fix-offscreen-bridge-midstream-persist-and-dedup-status and the re-verification of fix-agent-bridge-chat-panel-wiring both found that tests covered pre-click persistence and already-selected live events separately, but not the stitched selection handoff through OffscreenClient.handleAgentEvent().

rejectedObservations.observation: The selection-handoff regression-gap observation was reported in both round-2 reviews.
